growth_records.cite: Oshiki, Mamoru et al. Cultivation of Planktonic Anaerobic Ammonium Oxidation (Anammox) Bacteria Using Membrane Bioreactor. Microbes and Environments (2013). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME13077. [PMC4070702]
growth_records.method: not reported (planktonic cells confirmed by microscopy/particle size 0.9-1.37 um = 76% at 318 d; no mu quantified for Scalindua)
growth_records.snippet: Another MBR was operated for the cultivation of Ca. Scalindua sp. with nitrogen loading rates of 0.037-0.076 kgN m-3 d-1 ... stable nitrogen removal (0.022-0.067 kg-N m-3 day-1) ... stoichiometric ratios of DeltaNO2-/DeltaNH4+ and DeltaNO3-
growth_records.notes: Prokaryotic marine anammox (halophilic). Only volumetric N loading/removal and stoichiometric ratios measured; NO biomass-specific growth rate reported for Scalindua (mu_net only measured for Brocadia). Substrate rates VOLUMETRIC. Reactor scaled 2L->10L at day 179 (HRT 2->7 d).
